name: wifi-recon
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi-recon`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: `go install github.com/justuswilhelm/wifi-recon` then `wifi-recon -I <iface> -N <tag>` (e.g. `wifi-recon -I en0 -N home`); requires root/monitor-capable interface for tshark -I

examples.command: `go install github.com/justuswilhelm/wifi-recon` then `wifi-recon -I <iface> -N <tag>` (e.g. `wifi-recon -I en0 -N home`); requires root/monitor-capable interface for tshark -I

algorithms: unfiltered 5-second monitor-mode pcap capture via `tshark -I -i <iface> -w - -a duration:5` (no display/capture filters — indiscriminate 802.11 frame dump)
local subnet host enumeration via `arp-scan -l` (IP/MAC/OUI table)
geolocation stamp via external `locateme -l` binary (macOS CoreLocation, not WiFi BSSID->Google API in-tree)
result aggregation: raw per-tool output files + a recon_result.json summary, filed under timestamped directory
produces: raw pcap file (<iface>_tshark.pcap) of 5s monitor-mode capture
arp-scan text output (IP/MAC/vendor of local-subnet hosts)
recon_result.json summary aggregating all tool outputs/errors
novelty: none — thin wrapper. It shells out to tshark (no in-code probe/PMKID/EAPOL/beacon parsing — just an unfiltered 5s pcap dump that airodump-ng already produces in a far more structured form), arp-scan -l (standard local host enum), and a macOS-only `locateme` binary. No custom recon algorithms; no WiFi-specific parsing, filtering, or analytics in the Go code itself. The only marginal differentiator is bundling a location stamp with the pcap, which is a weak wardrive analogue and depends on a binary absent from Kali.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
